gallery.desc: These visitors to Fort Manuel take advantage of the high point to look out over the plains. Location: South Dakota (44.356° N 100.378° W) Status: Public domain.

A small bird sits atop the Standing Rock Monument overlooking beautiful Lake Oahe on the Missouri River. Location: North Dakota (45.871° N 100.442° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Deanne Bear Catches

gallery.cap: Bison on the Wide Open Prairie
gallery.desc: A small group of bison make their way across the flowing green prairie grass of the North Dakota plains. Location: North Dakota (46.237° N 100.624° W) Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Buffalo Herd at the Lakota Tribal Park
gallery.desc: These shaggy buffalo graze in the Lakota Tribal Park, an immense section of grasslands on the eastern side of the Cheyenne River Indian Reservation. Location: Lakota Tribal Park, Cheyenne River Indian Reservation, South Dakota (45.003° N 101.279° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Cheyenne River Sioux Tribe Game, Fish Parks Dept.
A golden-brown bird of prey soars over the Cheyenne River Indian Reservation Location: Cheyenne River Indian Reservation, South Dakota (45.008° N 101.234° W) Status: Public domain.

Byway visitors rest on these benches in the center of restored Fort Manuel Lisa. The outbuildings and living quarters were reconstructed to match historical descriptions. Location: Near Kenel, South Dakota (45.858° N 100.460° W) Status: Public domain.
Several beaver pelts line the table of the Fort Manuel Store, with other period trapping equipment decorating the wall behind. Location: South Dakota (45.858° N 100.460° W) Status: Public domain.
The Stockade is the last remaining building of Fort Yates, the largest military outpost along the Missouri River. Location: Fort Yates, North Dakota (46.092° N 100.629° W) Status: Public domain.
The morning mists obscure the shores of the Standing Rock Reservation along Four Mile Creek Location: North Dakota (46.035° N 100.630° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Mark White Bull
A small ethnography class gathers on the North Dakota prairie to learn more about the area. Location: North Dakota (46.232° N 100.647° W) Status: Public domain.
A Native American Welcome! Miss Standing Rock "Mary Eagle" extends a warm welcome to the Standing Rock Reservation. Location: North Dakota (45.860° N 100.461° W) Status: Public domain.

The Standing Rock Native American Scenic Byway takes you past two places where one of the greatest leaders in American history is said to be buried. Sitting Bull died along the Grand River in western Standing Rock December 15, 1890 and was subsequently buried at Fort Yates, North Dakota. This site is marked and located on the drive into Fort Yates, within one mile of the Standing Rock Native Ameri

A large part of Native American Heritage, this buffalo herd stretches in a near unbroken line between grass and sky. Buffalo have a great historical significance for the Lakota/Dakota people. Before the incursion of European settlers, bison provided the people with food, clothing and shelter. After many years of absence, the bison are once again on Standing Rock. Location: North Dakota (46.233° N
